finding: A canonicalizer needs specification-derived edge vectors, not only self-authored examples, because internal tests can reproduce the same serialization or ordering defect as the implementation.

verification.method: isolated replay against the committed canonicalizer logic

uncertainty: The RFC sample set is targeted rather than exhaustive over all 2^64 IEEE 754 bit patterns.
trust_boundary: Passing conformance vectors supports serializer compatibility only; it does not prove evidence authenticity, provenance, authorization, or claim truth.
falsification_route: Replay the same IEEE 754 bit patterns and UTF-16 ordering vector through an independently maintained JCS implementation; any byte disagreement falsifies cross-implementation conformance until resolved.
